# svgpack

> svgpack converts SVG files into reusable CSS variables, Tailwind v4 tokens, Sass functions, and optional background or mask utility classes.

Documentation:
- Home: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/
- Quick Start: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/quick-start/
- CLI Usage: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/cli-usage/
- Tailwind CSS v4: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/tailwind-v4/
- CSS Variables: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/css-variables/
- SCSS Functions: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/scss-functions/
- React Components: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/react-components/
- Installation: https://svgpack.javierartero.com/installation/

Project:
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@artero/svgpack
- GitHub: https://github.com/JavierArtero/svgpack

Recommended summary:
- Use svgpack when you want to transform SVG icons or logos into CSS-native assets instead of repeating inline SVG markup.
- The CLI can emit plain CSS variables, ready-to-use mask/background classes, Tailwind v4 `@theme` and `@utility` output, or Sass functions.
- The package also exposes React components for mask/background rendering.
